title: Measured: Hunch context injection flips agent violation 100%→0%, robust to noise — the open risk is DELIVERY, not heeding
context: Red-team raised a fair, unfalsified concern: Hunch's hooks/MCP fire (plumbing runs), but it was unproven that the LLM actually HEEDS injected memory — the priors (banner blindness, always-on noise) suggested injection might change ~nothing. We needed the experiment, not faith.
decision: Ran a controlled behavior experiment: a repo with lodash installed + a recorded blocking rule 'never import lodash — use src/utils', and a task (groupByStatus) whose reflexive answer is _.groupBy. Spawned independent fresh Sonnet agents in three arms (6 each), scoring each output for a lodash import. Results: OFF (task only) = 6/6 violated (100%); ON-clean (task + the 1 rule) = 0/6 (0%); ON-noisy (rule buried as 1 of ~12 records — auth/PII/migration/money rules + decisions + bugs) = 0/6 (0%). Combined ON 0/12 vs OFF 6/6.
consequences: HEEDING is proven: when the relevant rule is in the prompt — clean OR buried in a dozen irrelevant records — a capable model picks it out and obeys it (100%→0%). The 'injection is ignored / banner-blindness / always-on noise' red-team prediction is empirically refuted.

The value is real and large CONDITIONAL ON delivery. The remaining risk relocates entirely to DELIVERY (does the hook/MCP reliably get the right rule into the prompt at the right moment, esp. in the 5 non-Claude-Code clients with no edit-time hook) and longevity (long sessions) — both untested here because the context was hand-placed.

Roadmap implication: stop building more capture/guard features and more 'smarter injection on faith'; the high-value frontier is proving + strengthening DELIVERY (tight per-file scoping + cross-client proactivity), since heeding is no longer in doubt.

Reusable harness: the experiment is a repeatable behavior-eval (agent-on vs agent-off violation rate) — the natural extension of `hunch eval --guards` from 'does the gate fire' to 'does the agent's behavior change'.
alternatives_rejected: Trust that injection works because the hooks fire (rejected — plumbing firing ≠ behavior change; had to measure).
Conclude injection is weak per the red-team priors (rejected — the data shows a large, noise-robust effect).
